title: ToString() calls should not be redundant
description: "ToString()" calls should not be redundantAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEfindingclumsyWhy is this an issue?Invoking a method designed to return a string representation of an object which is already a string is a waste of keystrokes. Similarly, explicitly invokingToString()when the compiler would do it implicitly is also needless code-bloat.This rule raises an issue whenToString()is invoked:on astringon a non-stringoperand to concatenationon an argument tostring.FormatNoncompliant code examplevar s = "foo"; var t = "fee fie foe " + s.ToString(); // Noncompliant var someObject = new object(); var u = "" + someObject.ToString(); // Noncompliant var v = string.Format("{0}", someObject.ToString()); // Noncom
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
var s = "foo";
var t = "fee fie foe " + s.ToString(); // Noncompliant - s is already a string

var someObject = new object();
var u = "" + someObject.ToString(); // Noncompliant - implicit ToString() in concatenation
var v = string.Format("{0}", someObject.ToString()); // Noncompliant - Format calls ToString() implicitly
Console.WriteLine(someObject.ToString()); // Noncompliant - WriteLine calls ToString() implicitly
compliant:
var s = "foo";
var t = "fee fie foe " + s; // Compliant - no redundant ToString()

var someObject = new object();
var u = "" + someObject; // Compliant - implicit ToString()

int number = 42;
var str = number.ToString(); // Compliant - converting non-string to string

checks.message: ToString() call is redundant - concatenation and string.Format call ToString() implicitly

logic.detection_conditions: ToString() call in contexts where it's called implicitly (concatenation, string.Format, Console.WriteLine)
logic.triggering_conditions: source contains ToString() call in string concatenation or as argument to string.Format/Console.WriteLine
exclusion_conditions: Value types requiring explicit ToString() (int, long, double, float, DateTime, decimal)
never_trigger_on: Value type ToString() calls with format specifiers
logic.reasoning: In string concatenation and string.Format contexts, ToString() is called implicitly by the runtime. Explicitly calling it is redundant and clutters the code.
